name: Nyang Nyang / Nyang-Nyang / Pantai Nyang Nyang
distance_from_dps_airport_km: approx. 20-22 km (about 45 minutes by car from DPS Ngurah Rai Airport)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu: ~75-90 min by scooter; From Uluwatu (Suluban/Single Fin): ~15-20 min by scooter
wave_type: Right-hander reef break over coral; short, punchy walls with occasional barrel on take-off; channel paddle out at higher tides
skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (not suitable for beginners due to coral reef, currents and remoteness)
wave_height_range: 1-3 ft on small swells (waist to head high); can hold up to 1.5x overhead but tends to close out in bigger swells. Known to break ~1 ft bigger than Uluwatu on a south swell
best_season: Works on both seasons but considered a small/clean-swell spot; wet season (Oct-Apr / Nov-Apr) for bigger NW-wind-protected swells with light early winds; dry season mornings can also work when Uluwatu is too small. Best 'go-to' when other Bukit spots are flat or too small
best_tide: Mid to high tide (high tide essential to cover the reef); low tide exposes the reef and is unsafe
best_wind: Light offshore winds; N to NE winds preferred (it is sheltered from the typical SE trade winds that blow out the other Bukit spots) [uncertain]
swell_consistency_rating: 3/5 (works ~150+ surfable days per year but very tide and size dependent; only really firing on smaller clean swells)
wave_length_meters: approx. 50-150 m (164-492 ft cited by guides)
wave_speed_rating: medium to fast (punchy, short walls; can be slabby/closeout in bigger swells)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Moderate to high difficulty - shifty peaks, occasional barrel on take-off, freak sets from the open southern ocean; shallow reef at lower tides
secondary_peaks: Main right-hand peak with a channel; some inside reform on smaller days; Nunggalan Beach reef sits to the east as a separate/alternate peak [uncertain]
peak_crowd_level: 2-3/5 (crowd factor cited 7/10 by some guides on good days, but usually uncrowded due to long descent; gets busiest when Uluwatu is too small)
access: Long steep descent of approx. 510-600 stairs / 20-30 min hike down the cliffs OR drive the steep paved Jalan Pantai Nyang-Nyang road by scooter/car to the beach. Two entrances: West (newer, easier scooter access) and East via Nunggalan (steeper)
paddle_out_method: Paddle out via the channel on the right (north) side of the reef at higher tides; do not paddle over the reef at low tide
entry_fee: Parking ~IDR 5,000 (motorbike) / ~IDR 10,000-20,000 (car); beach entry effectively free though some warungs/operators may ask for a small donation [uncertain]
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol / early morning during rising tide to high tide; first light to mid-morning before onshore winds pick up
best_time_of_day: Before 9 am to avoid wind and crowds; sunset sessions also possible on glassy evenings
nearby_warung: Nyang Nyang Warung at the cliff top (Indonesian + Western); Loca Warung up on the main road; a handful of small warungs and coconut stalls on the beach itself; very limited compared to Uluwatu
alternative_spot_within_10min: Uluwatu, Padang Padang and Impossibles (~10-15 min scooter); Nunggalan reef next door; Green Bowl further south
hazards: Strong currents, freak/rogue sets from open southern ocean, exposed coral reef, urchins, occasional tiger shark sightings reported, no lifeguards, very remote with limited rescue access, steep unlit trail after dark, monkeys on the trail
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on the reef, occasional jellyfish, rare tiger shark sightings reported by locals (treat as low probability but real); standard coral cuts - flush with fresh water, disinfect, see clinic if deep
reef_cut_severity_index: High - live coral reef, sharp and shallow at lower tides; reef boots advisable on bigger or low-tide sessions
rip_current_pattern: Channel rip on the north/right side of the reef pulls outside (used as paddle channel); strong sweep along the reef during bigger swells and outgoing tides [uncertain]
lineup_etiquette: Standard Bukit reef etiquette - respect priority on the inside, do not snake locals, smile/say hi, wait your turn
localism_intensity: Low to medium (cited 3/10) - not a heavily localised peak, but a small core of regulars; respect goes a long way
respect_protocol: Greet locals, do not paddle around for sets, no shouting/dropping in, leave no trash, support local warungs/parking attendants
resident_local_legends: No single iconic local legend tied specifically to Nyang Nyang in the same way Mega Semadhi is to Padang; broader Bukit/Pecatu local crew surfs here [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Bukit cliffs near Uluwatu Temple area - dress modestly when off the beach; respect Nyepi (silent day) when no surfing/movement is allowed island-wide; menstruating women traditionally asked to avoid temple grounds
medical_nearby: Unicare Clinic Pecatu is the closest 24h clinic; BIMC Nusa Dua and Siloam Hospital Denpasar for serious injuries
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Unicare Pecatu approx. 6-8 km; BIMC Nusa Dua approx. 12-15 km [uncertain]
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated jet ski rescue on standby at this spot
emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; Bali Search and Rescue (BASARNAS) 115; Unicare Clinic Pecatu +62 361 8470770 [uncertain]
nearest_surf_school: No surf school operates directly at Nyang Nyang (too advanced/remote). Pecatu/Uluwatu area schools such as Padang Padang Surf Camp, Rapture Surfcamps Padang Padang and Uluwatu Surf Villas handle lessons in the broader area
board_rental_availability: No on-beach board rental at Nyang Nyang itself; rent in Uluwatu / Padang Padang / Pecatu before driving down
surf_photographer_available: No regular land/water photographer stationed at the lineup (unlike Uluwatu/Padang)
photographer_recommendation: Freelance Bukit-based water photographers can be hired by appointment via Instagram (e.g. local Uluwatu photographers); rates typically IDR 500,000-1,500,000 per session [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Six Senses Uluwatu (luxury, nearby), The Bubble Hotel Nyang Nyang (unique transparent domes on the beach), Cari Surf Camp Pecatu, plus numerous Pecatu/Uluwatu villas and homestays within 5-10 min drive
drone_allowed: Generally allowed - the wide empty beach is one of Bali's most droned locations; respect privacy of other beachgoers and avoid flying over temple grounds [uncertain]
coworking_nearby: No coworking on the beach; closest options are Tropical Nomad, Outpost or B Work in Canggu (~1+ hr away); some cafe-coworking in Bingin/Uluwatu
water_quality_rating: Generally good in dry season due to remoteness and low development; can carry runoff sediment in heavy wet-season rain; rated higher than Kuta/Seminyak [uncertain]
